checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: Accounts receivable roughly doubled from 2014 to 2015 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=26,672,000 at 2014-12-31 to 53,755,000 at 2015-12-31, +102%) while annual revenue grew only ~58% (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=118,223,000 in 2014 to 187,296,000 in 2015).

AR continued to outpace sales into 2016: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=63,427,000 (2016-09-30) versus quarterly SalesRevenueGoodsNet=64,429,000 (2016 Q3), implying receivables of roughly a full quarter of sales (DSO ~90 days) up from ~73 days in 2012 (AR=7,653,561 vs Q4-2012 rev=10,509,000).

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill was flat at 4,040,000 through 2015-12-31 then stepped to 30,730,000 (2016-03-31), and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill rose from 10,763,000 (2015-12-31) to 33,710,000 (2016-03-31); the step change is a single-quarter acquisition-driven jump rather than gradual organic capitalization outpacing the business.
OtherAssetsNoncurrent remained trivial (26,000 at 2015-09-30), showing no build-up of capitalized soft costs.

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: In 2015 net income substantially exceeded operating cash flow: NetIncomeLoss=29,446,000 (2015) versus NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=18,807,000 (2015), a ~10.6M gap consistent with earnings tied up in receivables.
In Q1 2016 the company reported positive earnings but negative operating cash flow: NetIncomeLoss=1,197,000 (2016 Q1) versus NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-977,000 (2016 Q1).

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margins remained stable and high (GrossProfit=167,094,000 on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=187,296,000 in 2015 = ~89%), consistent with a high-margin tissue business.

Inventory rose from InventoryNet=7,460,000 (2015-12-31) to 17,967,000 (2016-03-31), but this coincides with the same-quarter acquisition step-up in goodwill/intangibles rather than a divergence from sales.

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Revenue rose in a remarkably smooth, monotonic sequence (SalesRevenueGoodsNet quarterly 40,767,000 / 45,679,000 / 49,015,000 / 51,835,000 across 2015), but reported earnings were volatile, not smooth: NetIncomeLoss spiked to 13,378,000 in 2015 Q4 then fell to 1,197,000 (2016 Q1) and 1,975,000 (2016 Q2).

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Receivables are presented net (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent) with no separate allowance-for-doubtful-accounts or sales-returns reserve balance disclosed in the provided data, so reserve adequacy relative to the growing AR base cannot be directly assessed.
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent generally grew with the business (2,504,000 at 2014-12-31 to 6,664,000 at 2016-09-30), providing no clear evidence of a declining reserve.

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Multiple SEC staff correspondence items appear immediately before the cutoff: UPLOAD 2016-10-31, CORRESP 2016-11-30, and UPLOAD 2016-12-06, indicating an active SEC comment/review process on recent filings.

History shows repeated amendments (10-K/A on 2008-07-29, 2008-09-30, 2011-05-02, 2012-04-27, 2012-06-21, 2014-04-30) and a prior late filing (NT 10-Q 2009-02-18), together with a high frequency of 8-K filings.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: The dataset spans 2010-2016 with quarterly and annual granularity across revenue, receivables, cash flow, inventory, margins, balance-sheet and a full filing chronology, sufficient for trend and cross-check analysis (e.g., SalesRevenueGoodsNet and AccountsReceivableNetCurrent series through 2016-09-30).

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature/aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., channel-stuffing / bill-and-hold or shipping ahead of demand to distributors) inflates recorded sales and net income while the offsetting benefit accumulates in receivables rather than cash. Evidenced by AR growing ~2x versus ~58% revenue growth and DSO rising toward ~90-105 days.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=63,427,000 (2016-09-30) is nearly equal to a full quarter of sales SalesRevenueGoodsNet=64,429,000 (2016 Q3).

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Earnings quality deterioration: reported net income exceeds cash generation because revenue is booked but not collected, so accruals (receivables) drive the gap. Overstated income relative to underlying cash.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=29,446,000 (2015) exceeded NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=18,807,000 (2015); Q1 2016 NetIncomeLoss=1,197,000 while operating cash flow was -977,000.

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-reserving: allowances for doubtful accounts and/or sales-returns reserves may be understated relative to a rapidly growing and slower-collecting receivable base, keeping net AR and income higher than warranted (reserve detail not separately disclosed, so directional inference only).

rationale_evidence.quote: Receivables are reported net with no separate allowance/returns reserve disclosed while AR grew from 26,672,000 (2014-12-31) to 63,427,000 (2016-09-30).

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew ~102% in 2015 versus ~58% revenue growth, with DSO rising toward ~90-105 days (CL1)
Net income exceeded operating cash flow in 2015 (29.4M vs 18.8M) and Q1 2016 showed positive NI with negative operating cash flow (CL3)
Active SEC correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP) in Oct-Dec 2016 plus a history of repeated 10-K/A amendments (CL7)
